problem_structure.unknown: which clause of the compound customs condition actually binds and must be solved first
data: three folders sealed red for perishables, blue for transit goods, and black for dual-use materials
invoices, temperature logs, and phytosanitary certificates for perishables
transit manifests, waybills, and proof of bonded carrier status
a single dual-use sheet watermarked with a rifle over a wrench
problem_structure.condition: the consolidated instruction fuses three separate customs requirements into one sentence and demands all conditions be satisfied before the noon sailing

correct_approach: Stop treating the instruction as a single requirement; tear the compound condition along its natural clause boundaries, set aside the parts that do not bind, and test each clause alone to find which one actually stalls the clearance.
